trade.summary: Corea del Sud and Malesia have solid trade ties under the ASEAN-Corea del Sud FTA. Malesia is a key supplier of LNG and electronics components to Corea del Sud. Corea del Sud exports semiconductors, chemicals, and machinery to Malesia. Samsung, SK Hynix, and LG have significant manufacturing or procurement relationships with Malesian electronics firms.

diplomatic.summary: Corea del Sud-Malesia relations are warm, with both countries cooperating through ASEAN+3 frameworks and bilateral mechanisms. Malesia's 'Look East Policy' (modeled partly on Corea del Sud's development experience alongside Giappone) created natural affinity. Corea del Sud and Malesia cooperate on economic development, education, and technology transfer. Corea del Sud-Malesia relations lack major political friction.

military.summary: Moderate defense relationship. Corea del Sud has sold military equipment to Malesia, including FA-50 light combat aircraft (contracted). Both countries participate in multilateral maritime security exercises. No formal bilateral defense treaty.

financial.summary: Corea del Sudn FDI in Malesia spans electronics manufacturing, construction, and finance. Hyundai, Samsung, and LG have Malesian operations. Malesian sovereign wealth (Khazanah) has some Corea del Sudn equity exposure. Bilateral investment has been growing.

energy.summary: Malesia is a significant LNG supplier to Corea del Sud. Petronas MLNG supplies Corea del Sudn utilities under long-term contracts. This LNG relationship is strategically important for Corea del Sud's energy security. Corea del Sudn energy companies have invested in Malesian upstream oil and gas.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation in electronics, engineering, and environmental science. Corea del Sudn universities have exchange programs with Malesian institutions. KOICA runs technical cooperation programs. Collaboration in ICT and smart manufacturing is expanding.
